qualifying_org_definition: Any organization where the ABAWD performs unpaid (volunteer or in-kind) work; Missouri DSS policy manual section 1105.035.20 explicitly lists 'Unpaid work (volunteer or in-kind)' as a qualifying activity meeting the 80-hour/month ABAWD requirement. No state-mandated MOU, pre-approved provider list, or workfare assignment is required for the general volunteer lane. SkillUP enrollment is confirmed voluntary, not a prerequisite.
verification_instrument: ABAWD Agreement to Volunteer form (dss.mo.gov/employment-training-provider-portal/docs/abawd-volunteer-agreement.pdf) — a DSS-issued form housed on the Employment Training Provider portal, used to certify volunteer hours for ABAWD compliance. Policy manual (1105.035.20) also allows general documentation or direct contact with the agency providing volunteer activity to verify actual hours.
hours_formula_detail: Form text states '80 hours of Work/Training' per calendar month; no workfare allotment formula referenced.
accepts_outofstate_detail: The ABAWD Agreement to Volunteer form (Section 2) asks for agency name, address, phone, supervisor name/title, and supervisor signature — no requirement that the agency be Missouri-based or registered in-state. The policy manual sections and the form contain no geographic restriction on the volunteer organization. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) applies: any qualifying nonprofit is eligible absent a state restriction, so out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Missouri did not hold any active ABAWD county waivers in FY2025 and the OBBBA (effective November 1, 2025) eliminated the broad waiver authority; no MO waivers listed in FNA records for 2025–2029.
submission_deadline: unknown — form instructions say to scan/email to DSS.FSD.Agreements@dss.mo.gov or mail to FSD Resource Center (West Plains, MO); no explicit deadline stated on the form or in accessible policy manual pages.
notes:
Missouri is assessed Tier 1 clean: public-facing Missouri DSS guidance confirms volunteer work qualifies for the ABAWD 80-hour/month requirement without any mandatory prestep (SkillUP is offered as an optional path, not mandatory enrollment). Proof is submitted directly to the Family Support Division — a letter or statement from the volunteer organization certifying hours is accepted, consistent with a self-report lane. Confidence is medium rather than high because the detailed policy manual section (1105.035.15, which covers ABAWD verification specifics) is password-protected and could not be directly reviewed; no named state-specific verification form was identified in accessible sources. Missouri enforces ABAWD statewide in 2026 following the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (HR1, effective November 1, 2025), which removed prior waiver authority.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial multi-angle search attempting to refute the Tier 1 clean rating. Searches covered: (1) Missouri SNAP work registration intake requirements, (2) Missouri SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026 volunteer track, (3) Missouri SNAP ABAWD approved volunteer site list/nonprofit verification form, (4) Missouri ABAWD case manager/employment plan requirements, (5) DSS manual sections 1105.035.00/1105.035.20, (6) SkillUP mandatory enrollment question, (7) ABAWD Agreement to Volunteer form provenance. Key findings: Section 1105.035.20 is publicly accessible and explicitly lists 'Unpaid work (volunteer or in-kind)' as a qualifying activity meeting the 80-hour ABAWD requirement — this is direct policy-manual confirmation of the general volunteer lane with no prestep language. SkillUP is confirmed voluntary ('the program is entirely voluntary') per search results summarizing Missouri DSS. An 'ABAWD Agreement to Volunteer' form exists at dss.mo.gov/employment-training-provider-portal/docs/abawd-volunteer-agreement.pdf — this resolves verification_instrument from 'unknown' to a specific named DSS form. The form is encrypted/password-protected as a PDF so exact contents could not be read, but its presence on the provider portal and the policy manual's general 'documentation or contact with the agency' language confirm it is a certification instrument, not a prestep enrollment requirement. No evidence found of: a state-maintained approved volunteer site list, mandatory pre-enrollment before volunteer hours count, MOU requirement for nonprofits in the general lane, or an employment plan imposed by a case manager as a gating condition. Section 1105.035.15 (Exemptions and Verification) remains password-protected — still the one material gap that prevents full certainty. However, given direct cite to 1105.035.20 explicitly permitting volunteer/unpaid work with no prestep, plus identification of the verification instrument and confirmation that SkillUP is voluntary, confidence is raised from medium to high. The Tier 1 clean rating is upheld; no adversarial evidence found to refute it.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully extracted full text of the ABAWD Agreement to Volunteer form (08/2024) via pypdf. Key findings: (1) HOURS: Form title explicitly says '80 hours of Work/Training' per calendar month — confirms federal_80 formula, monthly_hours_required=80. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT: Form Section 2 (agency section) asks for agency name/address/phone and supervisor name/signature with no in-state registration or Missouri-domicile requirement; policy manual likewise silent on nonprofit geography — classified 'silent', meaning federal default applies and out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS: Missouri had no ABAWD county waivers in FY2025 (confirmed via FNA records); OBBBA eliminated broad waiver authority effective Nov 1, 2025 — fully enforced statewide. (4) SUBMISSION UX: Form Section 3 lists two options: (a) scan and email to DSS.FSD.Agreements@dss.mo.gov, (b) mail to FSD Resource Center, West Plains MO. Form also notes recipients can bring proof to local FSD office. No explicit monthly deadline stated. Cadence is monthly per the 80-hours-per-calendar-month standard. (5) FORM FIELDS: Section 1 (recipient): name, DCN (case/client number), phone, DOB, address, signature. Section 2 (agency): job title/position description, volunteer start date, projected hours/month, agency name/address/phone, supervisor name/title, supervisor signature. Mapped to schema: org_name, org_address, org_phone, org_signature, supervisor_name, recipient_name, recipient_case_number, recipient_DOB, recipient_address, hours_total, activity_description, period_start_date. Note: no EIN field on this form; no notarization required; no hours_by_day_or_week breakdown (only monthly total); period_end_date not explicitly present (only start date + projected hours).
